objective: Deliver the source-only Rusty Fleet foundation as one coherent vertical stack: accepted product and operator-projection boundaries; versioned identity, condition, query, saved-view, fleet-row, inspector, detail, operation-ledger, and command-lifecycle contracts; damaged and representative-scale fixtures; a deterministic multi-device simulator; an in-memory Hub state engine; and CLI/local-API parity without opening sockets, WPF, platform permissions, ADB, media, relay, or device work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the landed source/build layout, focused checks, and unchanged authority boundaries.
Replace planning-only setup with build, simulation, CLI, and local API entrypoints.
Register the selected implementation toolchain, focused component checks, and Milestone 0 scenario suite.
Review whether the new public Hostess/operator product changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged if existing lane rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record the result before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether the fleet directory, projection, and simulator contracts add reusable authority guidance; retain unchanged if existing rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and record the result before acceptance.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the initial source-root and instruction graph routing after implementation lands.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run the bounded tracked-file inventory and record the review before acceptance.

non_scope: Real network listeners, discovery, BLE, LSL, ZeroMQ, Manifold transport, persistence service, online relay, or cloud deployment.
Quest, Android, OpenXR, Spatial SDK, APK, permissions, foreground observation, package lifecycle, ADB, File Manager, Kiosk mutation, or device validation.
Media sources, processors, sockets, routes, codecs, sinks, frames, audio, meshes, depth, or high-rate tracking.
Feature activation, runtime effects, private payloads, production credentials, or local machine evidence.
Separate lifecycle units for individual schemas, fixtures, model types, CLI commands, tests, documentation edits, or repairs discovered inside this milestone.

acceptance.proof: Public product-owned contracts define device identity, independent status conditions, status source, status/capability snapshots, staleness, command lifecycle, per-device results, and audit projections with explicit owners, bounds, provenance, freshness, and unknown-field policy.
acceptance.command: Run the Milestone 0 contract and damaged-fixture suite.

acceptance.proof: Versioned canonical query, saved-view, summary-count, fleet-row, inspector, full-detail, and operation-ledger projections preserve source, age, accepted authority, result revision, scope, and CLI/local-API equivalence without defining WPF-only state.
acceptance.command: Run the Milestone 0 projection, query, saved-view, and parity fixtures.

acceptance.proof: One deterministic scenario runner creates and evolves 4, 50, 250, 1,000, and 5,000-device datasets, including reconnect, reordering, replay, duplicate identity, staleness, offline state, capability downgrade, partial families, and damaged messages. Fixture size is not a supported-scale claim.
acceptance.command: Run the Milestone 0 simulator scenario suite.

acceptance.proof: The in-memory Hub advances accepted device state only through valid identity/revision/freshness transitions and preserves independent device outcomes under concurrent simulated check-in.
acceptance.command: Run the Fleet Hub unit and property tests.

acceptance.proof: List, inspect, filter, and watch operations from fleetctl and the local API produce the same canonical projections and error vocabulary.
acceptance.command: Run the Milestone 0 CLI/local API parity suite.

acceptance.proof: No socket, Android, Quest, ADB, Kiosk, File Manager, media, relay, permission, service, route, stream, marker, device, or runtime effect is selected or introduced.
The milestone remains one reviewable stack, public files contain only synthetic evidence, instruction surfaces are synchronized, and the repository Standard gate passes.

outputs: Versioned product-owned identity, condition, query, saved-view, fleet-row, inspector, detail, operation-ledger, and command-lifecycle schemas with valid, boundary, damaged, replay, staleness, and downgrade fixtures.
Deterministic 4, 50, 250, 1,000, and 5,000-device simulator datasets and an in-memory Fleet Hub state engine.
fleetctl and local API list, inspect, filter, and watch parity.
Milestone scenario suite, diagnostics, public documentation, and rollback evidence.
commit_policy: Commit coherent internal layers such as contracts plus fixtures, engine plus tests, and projections plus parity evidence. Do not create file-sized lifecycle units or preserve meaningless intermediate breakage. Accept and publish only after the complete milestone Standard gate passes.
